required_fields: Answer six fit-check questions: Texas-based status, Medicaid/Medicare status, immediate safety risk, recent hospitalization, age 18+, virtual-care preference
optional_fields: Go back link available during fit check
error_state_tested: Limited branch testing only. Selected Texas Yes, Medicaid/Medicare No, immediate safety risk No, recent hospitalization No, 18+ Yes, virtual care Yes.
possible_friction: The fit check adds safety and eligibility clarity before the main intake, but it creates a pre-intake stage before the user reaches provider matching.
hypothesis: This may improve safety and routing quality, but some users may perceive the booking process as longer before they see providers or availability.
notes: The fit-check flow asks six questions before the main intake. It screens for Texas eligibility, Medicare/Medicaid, immediate safety risk, recent hospitalization, adult age, and virtual-care preference. Copy emphasizes safety and appropriate care resources.

optional_fields: Multiple conditions can be selected; I'm not sure and Other are available
error_state_tested: Selected Other without description during walkthrough and saw a validation error requiring more detail. Then unselected Other.
possible_friction: The user must self-classify their mental health need before seeing the full care path. Condition-specific details appear only after clicking a card.
hypothesis: Patients with overlapping symptoms may hesitate or select multiple options because they may not know which category best fits their need.
notes: Step 1 of 6 and 17% complete. Options shown include ADHD, Depression, Anxiety, Bipolar Disorder, PTSD, I'm not sure, and Other. Depression and Anxiety were selected during the walkthrough. The Anxiety card displayed a medication-related restriction note. The I'm not sure option reduces classification pressure. The Other option requires a written description.

optional_fields: Other Insurance and No Insurance are available
payment_or_billing_step: Insurance provider requested; no card or payment requested yet
error_state_tested: Branch tested United Healthcare and No Insurance. Selecting No Insurance displayed self-pay pricing.
possible_friction: The insurance step is clear, but users who do not know whether their exact plan is accepted may still feel uncertainty before seeing provider match, appointment availability, or final cost.
hypothesis: Insurance selection at this stage may create hesitation for users who are unsure about coverage, local plan details, or out-of-pocket cost exposure.
notes: Step 2 of 6 and 33% complete. Visible options include Aetna, Blue Cross Blue Shield, Cigna, United Healthcare, Oscar Health, Other Insurance, and No Insurance. Selecting No Insurance displayed a self-pay note: $250 for new patient intakes and $150 for follow-ups. The self-pay copy is a strength because it gives pricing clarity.

error_state_tested: Did not test all branches deeply. Selected I'm looking for a new diagnosis or medication.
possible_friction: The user must choose between diagnosis and medication-support pathways. Users who are unsure about whether they need diagnosis, medication, or general support may hesitate.
hypothesis: This step helps route patients, but the wording is diagnosis and medication oriented. That may create uncertainty for users who know they need help but do not know the correct clinical path.
notes: Step 3 of 6 and 50% complete. A green banner appeared saying the account had been created and the user could continue later by logging back in. Options shown: I already have a diagnosis and need medication support; I'm looking for a new diagnosis or medication; I'm not sure, but I need help. The selected option was I'm looking for a new diagnosis or medication.

required_fields: Review matched providers and available appointment times
optional_fields: Show more provider bio; show more providers; optional earlier-appointment checkbox after selecting a slot
payment_or_billing_step: Insurance or self-pay context influences matching; no card or payment requested yet
error_state_tested: Limited branch testing: changed insurance and care-pathway selections; visible top provider list appeared largely similar across combinations.
possible_friction: Provider match results are useful, but there is no clearly visible edit control for changing earlier answers from the match screen. Similar visible results across different input combinations may reduce perceived personalization.
hypothesis: If users expect provider matches to change based on insurance, condition, or care pathway, seeing similar providers may weaken trust in the matching logic or make labels like perfect match and highly recommended feel less transparent.
notes: Step 4 of 6. The page shows provider cards with clinician name, Legion clinician badge, star rating, role, areas of expertise, bio preview, earliest availability, and appointment slots. Labels observed include Soonest available, Highly recommended, and Perfect match found. Visible providers included Georgia Dixon, Similoluwa Olushola, and Chinwe Mbonu. Branch testing was limited and may be affected by provider overlap, session state, location, supply constraints, or account caching.

required_fields: Select an appointment time to continue booking
optional_fields: Optional checkbox: I would like an earlier appointment if one becomes available
payment_or_billing_step: No payment or card requested yet
error_state_tested: Selected a visible time slot but did not finalize booking.
possible_friction: After selecting a slot, the CTA changes into a booking-specific button. This is clear, but clicking it likely moves the user toward real booking details, so the audit stopped before submitting personal or payment information.
hypothesis: The selected slot creates strong booking momentum, but the user still needs confidence in provider fit, cost, and appointment details before continuing.
notes: A Georgia Dixon slot was selected for Wednesday Jun 24 at 6:00pm. The page displayed a button: Book appointment for Wednesday 24th June at 06:00pm. A note said the first appointment will last approximately 1 hour. The walkthrough stopped before submitting personal details or payment information.

optional_fields: Multiple appointment times across visible dates
error_state_tested: Compared visible availability labels across provider cards.
possible_friction: Availability language may be ambiguous. Some cards say times in the next X days, but visible dates can span beyond a simple calendar-day interpretation.
hypothesis: If next X days means next X available booking days, the interface should say that. Otherwise users may think the availability count is inaccurate.
notes: One provider card showed 4 times in the next 2 days with dates that appeared consistent. Another provider card showed 15 times in the next 3 days while visible dates included later calendar dates. This should be framed as ambiguous copy, not a confirmed bug.
